The cormorant eats exclusively marine and freshwater fish. It lives near the coast and loves large bodies of water where it can dive for food.
The Japanese cormorant, a subspecies of cormorant, is also known as the Temminck's cormorant. It is native to East Asia, from northern Taiwan to Korea and Japan.
Cormorants are very good divers. They hunt underwater and have webbed feet that allow them to propel themselves forward underwater.
Cormorants lay their eggs in colonies with thousands of other breeding pairs. They build their nests on hard-to-reach cliffs along the coast or inland in tall trees.
The cormorant has a hooked beak with which it catches its prey. It is a rival to any fisherman!
Green dragonfly larvae can catch prey with their mouthparts. Adult dragonflies catch mosquitoes and flies in flight.
Greenish-green sedges are found in many places. They feel at home in Taiwan and around watery and marshy areas.
Green-winged spiders are arthropods. Their delicate legs are dark gray to black in color.
Dragonflies have two lives. First as larvae in the water and then in full glory as dragonflies. Frost or pollution in the water do not have much effect on the larvae.
Dragonflies move their wings independently. Not only can they suddenly change direction, but they can also fly backwards.
Ocelots hunt over a vast territory. They will eat basically anything they can find, but they prefer small prey such as rodents and rabbits.
Ocelots are found in Central America and the Amazon rainforest. They live in areas with a high amount of vegetation cover, but can also appear in more open areas at night.
Ocelots are active animals that roam over vast territories of up to 18 square kilometers. They are found at altitudes of up to 1200 meters.
They usually have only one young, which lives with its mother for up to two years.
Unlike other cat species, ocelots are not afraid of water and are excellent swimmers.
The wild boar has a very flexible upper lip, which allows it to graze even in meadows with shorter grass.
Yaks live in the Himalayas, Mongolia, and even southern Siberia. In Tibet, people use their wool, meat, skin, milk, and even their dung, which is used as fuel.
Yak hooves are quite small and strong. They have sharp tips and hard edges that allow them to descend more slowly down hills. This allows them to walk safely even on rough terrain.
Baby yaks can stand on their feet five to fifteen minutes after birth, after which they immediately seek their mother's udder.
Yaks like to move around. While grazing, they sometimes take a break and play fetch.
Coatis use their snouts to search for small invertebrates. They also eat fruit for dessert.
Coatis live in tropical rainforests and are widespread from the USA to Argentina.
Coatis scurry along the ground on their black paws, searching for food. At night, they climb trees to sleep.
The young are born high in the treetops. During this period, the males search for small animals and fruit to protect their young from predators.
Their flexible snout reaches into all sorts of holes, nooks and crannies, where they catch their prey.
Fruit bats love to feast on nectar, pollen, and fruit, but during Halloween, these flying foxes come to life and swoop down on their victims!
As pets of the infamous Count Dracula, they can be seen all over Romania. Otherwise, they live in Africa, Asia, or Australia.
Fruit bats are distinguished from common bats by their claws on the second finger, which have a membranous structure extending over the fingers.
A fruit bat can live up to 30 years. They give birth to a young one once a year.
Like werewolves, fallow deer like to drink blood sometimes. They can even smell fresh blood, uuuuaaaaaaaahhhhhh!
Kashmiri goats enjoy grass, making them ideal for maintaining the landscape. The only problem is that they smell pretty bad.
The Kashmir goat is originally from Tibet, but nowadays it can also be seen in Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan.
Kashmiri goats hop across the mountains and are not cold at all. They feel good in the steppes, even in extreme climatic conditions.
Kashmiri goats have such fluffy, soft wool that you would love to cuddle them. Their large horns are sometimes hidden under their long ears.
Weavers all over the world admire this wool goat. A nice sweater made from cashmere is very luxurious and warm.
There are many species of birds of paradise. It is said that their appearance is determined by their diet. They all enjoy fruit, but some are very specialized, eating nutmeg, for example.
The bird of paradise is on the national emblem of Papua New Guinea. Birds of paradise got their name from the sailors who discovered the worlds there - a true paradise in the middle of the ocean!
Male birds of paradise perform an interesting mating dance to attract females. Hmmmm... would you like to dance too?
They are extravagant, rash and incredibly colorful - birds of paradise are thrill seekers. Some are monogamous and others are polygamous - variety is the spice of life.
The trademark of this bird is its magnificent plumage. Its tail feathers stand out and its body is like a dazzling mosaic of colors.
Seagulls love fish the most. But they will pounce on anything that looks tasty! They often take pizza, chocolate or ice cream from tourists.
Gulls are abundant along the northern coast of Germany. They hunt worms, crustaceans and molluscs in the Wadden Sea. Every tide brings them fresh fish.
Clap, clap! They have three webbed toes on each foot, which makes it easier for them to move across the muddy sand left behind at low tide.
The chicks are kept warm by thick down feathers. As they grow, their feathers change to those found in adults. Gulls can live up to 30 years!
During a coastal storm, seagulls can glide beautifully over the water in strong winds. That's beautiful!
Night has fallen, it's raining... the Cape cobra emerges from its burrow to hunt small mammals and reptiles. Grab it and it's gone!
This small snake lives in the sandy semi-desert of South Africa, where it feels warm and comfortable under the sand.
The Cape ladder crawls its way through warm or damp sand, leaving light tracks behind. Isn't that cute?
In summer, the Cape cobra lays 3 to 11 eggs, which it incubates in its hole.
The Cape ladder digs into the ground like a drill. When threatened, it straightens and inflates its neck, but its venom is not very strong.
The Bércoun most enjoys eating insects, spiders, and centipedes.
The African wildcat feels at home in Africa... except in the western region. For some reason, it hasn't jumped there yet.
Short legs and a funny gait are its most distinctive features. Who would have thought it could run so fast?
They grow up very quickly... In just 6-10 weeks they will leave their parents and establish their own home.
The Bércoun is monogamous and stays with its chosen partner for the rest of its life. That's romantic!
The striped mongoose feeds on small insects, reptiles, and birds, which it picks from the ground and trees with its long claws.
The savannas and grasslands in the central-eastern zone of Africa stretching from Eritrea provide them not only with a home but also with abundant prey.
The striped mongoose lives in a variety of hiding places. It makes its home in rocks, bushes, gorges, and even termite mounds!
The striped mongoose is very social and lives in groups of about 20 individuals. Both males and females live together and have a hierarchy based on age.
The pack is matriarchal and all members are taken care of, especially the young and old.
Crabs feed primarily on algae, but they will also feast on mollusks, worms, sponges, and even other crustaceans.
Crabs are found in every ocean in the world, both in fresh water and on land. They have even been found in smoking volcanic vents or climbing trees!
Most crabs walk sideways due to the direction of their leg joints, but some move forward or backward.
The hard shell of the crab prevents them from growing, so young crabs must molt 6 or 7 times in their first year. As adults, they only molt once or twice a year.
Crabs use their claws for a variety of purposes including nutrition, defense, and mating. If they lose a claw or leg, they will grow new ones!
The blue-footed booby is quite picky about fish. Its favorite foods are sardines, anchovies, and mackerel.
The blue-footed booby is found along the Pacific coast of Central America. It is a purely marine bird that uses land for breeding.
The blue legs play a role during the courtship ritual. Males display their legs to females, who then evaluate them and choose their mate.
Its name comes from the Spanish word "bobo", which means clumsy or stupid, because that's how it moves on land.
The blue-footed booby is a large bird. The female is larger than the male and can grow up to 90 cm tall and have a wingspan of up to 1.5 meters!
A nice collection of Easter eggs. In the past, painted chicken eggs were used. Nowadays, chocolate eggs are more common.
Previously, painting was not only done on chicken eggs, but also on ostrich eggs, and even an ostrich egg that was 60,000 years old was found!
Egg hunts are a popular tradition around the world. Children win prizes for collecting the most eggs or finding the smallest or largest egg.

In some Slavic countries, it is a custom to symbolically whip the backs of girls and women from the neighborhood and pour water over them, for which boys win ribbons and eggs. It is said that this will increase their beauty and health.
Egg dancing is a traditional game in which eggs are placed on the ground and people try to dance around them without breaking any.
Easter eggs specially adapted for visually impaired children to participate in the Easter hunt. The eggs beep or play a melody.
The dromedary mainly eats desert plants. It can store up to 36 kilos of fat in its hump, which gradually decomposes and allows it to survive in the desert.
The dromedary has not existed in the wild for 2,000 years, but occasionally a stray one can be found.
Dromedaries usually roam freely in herds of up to 20 individuals, mostly females led by a single male.
After a 15-month gestation period, a single calf is born. The female cares for the calf for two years.
The dromedary has adapted to life in the desert and can tolerate more than 30% water loss. This means that it only needs to drink once every 10 to 15 days.
Like other antelopes, the greater kudu likes to feast on leaves and grass and sometimes even tangerines and oranges.
The greater kudu roams throughout southern Africa. It likes populated areas and seeks food and safety in forests, abandoned fields and pastures.
The horns of a greater kudu are fully curled by the age of six. If they were straightened, they would measure 120 cm or more!
The greater kudu usually has only one young, which hides in the vegetation from predators for several weeks.
Despite its size, the greater kudu is a good jumper and can even jump over a 3-meter fence!
The spotted woodpecker is a solitary predator that hunts insects, small mammals, birds, and snakes at night. It also eats vegetables and fruits.
The spotted kunoichi is mainly known in Tasmania, and an attempt to reintroduce it into the wild has recently begun in Australia.
Each spotted marten has up to 5 different dens, which it lives in alternately. Each den has several dug holes.
Civets are marsupials, which means that females care for their young in a pouch on their front.
The kunovci are black or golden brown in color, but all have distinctive white dots on their backs.
Dolphins feed on fish and squid, but larger dolphin species such as killer whales feed on smaller mammals.
Most dolphins prefer warmer tropical waters, but some species of dolphin can also be found in cold waters. Some dolphins have even found their way into rivers.
The dolphin is very active and social. Its tail fin allows it to make long leaps above the surface.
The young are usually born in spring and summer and stay with their mother for a long time.
Dolphins are very intelligent. They learn, cooperate and grieve together.
The sperm whale dives to a depth of 2 kilometers, where it searches for medium-sized squid. It also feeds on octopuses and fish.
The sperm whale lives in the ocean. Like all whales, the sperm whale must surface to breathe.
The sperm whale has the largest brain of any animal in the world - five times larger than a human brain!
Sperm whales reproduce slowly. Females give birth once every 4 to 20 years and care for their young for up to 10 years.
Each sperm whale tooth weighs up to 1 kilo, but they are not needed to catch or eat squid.
The giant squid feeds on deep-sea fish or smaller squid, capturing its prey with its long tentacles.
The giant squid lives deep in the ocean, which is why it grows to such large sizes.

The giant squid has the largest eye of any living creature, measuring up to 27 cm in diameter with a pupil of up to 9 cm. Thanks to its eyes, it can detect even the slightest light on the ocean floor.
Genetic research has shown that there is little difference between octopuses, suggesting that there is only one species in the world.
The inside of the tentacles is covered with suction cups, which are lined with so-called "teeth". Thanks to them, it repels predators, especially sperm whales.
Marlin feed on fish such as mackerel and tuna. They use their long, sword-like tentacles to catch fish.
Marlin prefer warmer water near the surface. They are migratory and follow warm water currents.
Fishermen must prepare for a great struggle when fishing. Writer Ernest Hemingway writes about one such struggle in his book "The Old Man and the Sea."
Females are much larger than males - up to 4 times! They lay up to a million eggs at a time, but only one of them reaches adulthood.
Blue marlin is one of the fastest fish in the ocean and can reach speeds of up to 100 km per hour!
The clownfish feeds mainly on plankton in the water, but will also enjoy the undigested food of its hosts.
Clownfish prefer warm, shallow water around coral reefs and shallow lagoons. They need anemones to survive!
Before the clownfish swims into the anemone, it dances in front of it to acclimatize. The anemone provides it with food and safety. In return, the clownfish removes parasites from it.
All clownfish are born male at birth. They have the ability to change their sex to become the leader female in the group.
The clownfish is the most popular aquarium fish, especially after becoming famous in Hollywood. Due to their hunting, clownfish are losing their population in the wild.
Jellyfish feed on plankton, eggs, crustaceans, and even other jellyfish - basically anything their tentacles can grab.
Jellyfish are ubiquitous. They live in every sea, ocean, and lake in the world. It is estimated that there are over 10,000 different species.
Jellyfish are invertebrates, meaning they have no bony structure. If a jellyfish is cut in two, it can regenerate into two new organisms - just like an earthworm.
Jellyfish lay eggs every day. The egg develops into a larva, which then develops into a polyp - a mouth opening surrounded by tentacles. Over time, the polyp becomes a jellyfish.
Jellyfish are almost entirely made of water, with certain species containing between 95 and 98% water.
The four-toothed catfish prefers invertebrates and algae. Large four-toothed cats can crack open clams, mussels, and clams with their large beaks.
Most species live near the coast and estuaries of marine waters, but a few species live exclusively in freshwater.
The quadruped moves very slowly, but is capable of sprinting at breakneck speeds. It has excellent eyesight, which helps it escape predators and prepare for defense.
Reproduction occurs on the surface of the water. The males push the females to the surface and the eggs float away.
The four-toothed fish inflates itself by filling its stomach with water. It sticks out its spines, making itself less attractive to predators.
Don't believe all those tales that the kraken feasts on lost sailors. The Kraken in Waterworld has fish on its menu.
The Kraken lives on the ocean floor, from where it emerges to capture its prey. It prefers the cold waters around Norway and Greenland.
The exact size of the kraken is unknown, but its tentacles can wrap around an entire ship, suggesting it is much larger than a giant squid.
There is speculation that the kraken does not reproduce and that only two individuals exist. Both were reportedly sighted in the same location in the Greenland Sea.
Like the giant squid, the kraken has large eyes with which it searches for prey. Encountering one in the deep ocean would be terrifying for any diver!
Dress warmly because it is quite cold here. Ice masses can be found at both poles, where they form the "land" of vast expanses of ice.
For hundreds of years, people have fished by drilling a hole in the ice. With this drill, you can remove the ice ring and then put it back in place.
Skating began in Finland 3,000 years ago. Since then, skating has become popular all over the world. People skate not only for fun, but it is also a practical way to get from place to place.
The first ice machine was built in the 19th century as a means of cooling hospital rooms. Before long, people were putting ice cubes in their drinks.

Only a tenth of the iceberg is visible above the water surface. The tip of an iceberg can be up to 75 meters high above the water surface. This means that the largest icebergs can be up to 750 meters underwater!
Almost every animal in the world (including humans) prefers to bathe in water during hot weather.
When boats are tied together, these plastic protectors are needed to be placed between them so that they don't constantly bang into each other and cause damage.
Underwater equipment accumulates deposits containing dirt, rust and even crustaceans! The equipment must therefore be cleaned regularly to prevent it from breaking.
Fishing nets have been used for thousands of years. Although the material they are made of has changed over time, the principle remains the same.
This nautical knot is simple but very useful for tying boats to poles. It is also commonly used in mountaineering.
We usually imagine pirates as described by Robert Louis Stevenson in his book Treasure Island.
People usually imagine a pirate with an eye patch. According to the book, other accessories include a wooden leg and a parrot.
This hook is used to attach a rope to another object. Pirates use it to capture an enemy ship, which they then pull to get on board.
Pirates only fly a jolly roger flag when they plan to attack another ship. The most famous flag depicts a skull and crossbones, but originally each pirate captain had their own design.
The map marked X, which leads to the treasure, comes from the book Treasure Island.
The largest and most famous is the Great Barrier Reef off Australia, but coral reefs can be found in warmer seas and oceans around the world.
Coral reefs are home to 25% of marine life. In an effort to preserve nature, artificial reefs have been built around the world from various materials, such as old tires.
Beautiful jewelry can be made from coral material. In the interest of coral conservation, jewelry has been made less recently.
Corals are not plants, but invertebrate animals (like jellyfish). Corals secrete calcium carbonate, which forms a solid skeleton, and together they build reefs.
Coral has been mined for centuries and is used for various purposes. However, over-mining threatens corals, which is why they are internationally protected.
The Caribbean is the region east of Mexico that includes seas and islands. There are over 700 islands in the Caribbean, and they are all beautiful.
The Caribbean is located in a tropical region where trade winds blow, making this climate suitable for all kinds of plant species.
The Caribbean is sunny and warm all year round, with minimal temperature variations, making it a popular destination for people from all over the world.
This hypothetical triangle stretches from Miami to San Juan and Bermuda. Since the 1950s, the region has been associated with mysterious phenomena involving the disappearance of ships and planes.
Coconut palms are found in tropical regions around the world. Coconuts are used for many purposes. And most importantly, they taste good!
For centuries, people have been diving and searching for food and treasure underwater. Diving equipment has undergone many innovations.
The deeper you dive, the darker it gets! These waterproof lights provide visibility in deep waters and watery caves.
Various remote-controlled machines are used during diving operations. They have lights, cameras, and detection or other devices attached to them. They are usually used to explore new areas.
Although this hook is associated with pirates, it was previously used as a replacement for amputated hands, most famously worn by Captain Hook from the film Peter Pan.
The Titanic sank on its maiden voyage in 1912. The wreck was found in 1985, and thousands of artifacts have been recovered since then.
Some killer whales hunt large fish such as salmon and herring, while others hunt sharks and rays.
Killer whales are found all over the world, but they are most common around Scandinavia, the North Pacific, and around Antarctica.
Despite years of research, little is known about the movements and migrations of killer whales. Killer whales travel all over the world, making them difficult to track.
Killer whales can live up to 90 years, similar to humans. Females give birth to one calf approximately every 5 years.
After Hollywood showed everyone the life of killer whales in captivity, there was huge pressure to release them back into the wild.
The seahorse feeds on small crustaceans. Thanks to its excellent camouflage skills, it is able to ambush its unsuspecting prey.
The seahorse is most often found in shallow water or coral reefs.
The seahorse measures 1.5 to 35 cm. It moves using its dorsal fin.
Courtship between seahorses lasts for several days, after which the female deposits her eggs in a pouch on the male's body. The male cares for them until they hatch, then releases them.
The seahorse uses its tail as a counterweight to maintain an upright posture. It also uses it to grab onto plants or coral tendrils to ambush its prey.
Bonobos prefer to enjoy fruit, but as omnivores they also like leaves, honey, eggs, small animals, or insects.
Bonobos are an endangered species and are found only in the Congo. Unfortunately, they face many human threats, such as hunting.
Like chimpanzees, bonobos are closely related to humans, as can be seen in their hands with four fingers and a thumb.
Bonobos live in a matriarchal society. Females give birth every 5 to 6 years and care for their young for up to 5 years.
Bonobos are the only animals besides humans that mate face to face and enjoy sexual intercourse for pleasure as well as reproduction.
Cockatoos mainly enjoy seeds, fruits and vegetables. Thanks to their strong beaks, they can open seeds.
Cockatoos are found in Australasia. They can be found in a variety of habitats – each species has its own preferred home.
Cockatoos share many characteristics with other parrot species, such as strongly curved beaks and zygodactyl feet.
Cockatoos are monogamous and mating pairs remain together for many years. Unlike many birds, they do not start mating until they are several years old and they only care for their young for a year.
The cockatoo raises its magnificent crest when landing after a flight and during courtship. Both males and females have crests.
Like other antelopes, the greater kudu likes to feast on leaves and grass and sometimes even tangerines and oranges.
The greater kudu roams throughout southern Africa. It likes populated areas and seeks food and safety in forests, abandoned fields and pastures.
Despite its size, the greater kudu is a good jumper and can even jump over a 3-meter fence!
The greater kudu usually has only one young, which hides in the vegetation from predators for several weeks.
The horns of a greater kudu are fully curled by the age of six. If they were straightened, they would measure 120 cm or more!
Owls are excellent nocturnal hunters and feed mostly on small mammals and insects, which they can see thanks to their excellent night vision.
Owls are found throughout the world, although many species prefer the temperate climates of Europe and North America.
The smallest owl measures 13.5 cm and the largest has a wingspan of up to 2 meters!
Females are considerably larger than males. They stay in the nest while the males hunt.

Owls have 14 cervical vertebrae (compared to 7 in humans), which allows them to turn their heads up to 270 degrees. Their eyes are firmly set in their sockets, so they must turn their heads to change their direction of vision.
Mantas suck up food while moving. They consume large amounts of plankton, consuming around 13% of their body weight each day.
Manta rays are found in temperate, subtropical, and tropical waters around the world. They migrate across the oceans both singly and in groups.
They have different swimming behaviors in different waters – in the deep oceans they swim in one plane, but near the coast they like to float on the water.
Mantas have a long gestation period of up to 13 months. After birth, the young resemble a miniature adult and do not require parental care.
Their body resembles a blanket, allowing them to blend in with the bottom. They can reach a width of up to 4 meters!
Cambodia's tropical forests are in grave danger. Deforestation is destroying the home of sun bears.
The Cambodian flag has two blue and one red stripes. The Angkor Wat temple is depicted in the center.
Don't be afraid of this print. The Malayan bear is an omnivore. It likes insects and of course honey!
This plush toy looks just as cute as a real Sun Bear! It has fewer teeth and claws than the real thing, so you can cuddle it!
Tetrameles is a famous tree that grows in Cambodia. It is famous worldwide mainly because it grows over ancient ruins, which adds to the mysterious and magical feel of the place.
The Kakapo has a very small stomach compared to other birds of its size. It is exclusively herbivorous and mainly enjoys seeds, fruits, plants and pollen.
The Kakapo is native to New Zealand, and the remaining birds live on these islands. They are threatened by the introduction of non-native species such as cats and ferrets, and by overhunting.

The Kakapo is part of the psittaciformes family. It is one of the heaviest parrots and the only flightless one. It is also probably one of the longest-living birds in the world, with a lifespan of up to 120 years!

Due to their long lifespan, the kakapo is one of the few animals, other than humans, that does not start breeding until they are in their teens. Females usually start looking for males when they are around 9 to 11 years old.
The Kakapo is known for its pleasant scent, which is similar to honey and pollen.
The beautiful bird of paradise travels through the forest hunting for prey. While it feeds on fruit and insects, it will also feast on frogs, small reptiles, or other birds.
The beautiful bird of paradise can be seen in the rainforests of New Guinea in the treetops.
Males are black with a green crown and breast and extravagant plumage. Females are reddish-brown and hoodless. During courtship, the male spreads his hood and dances around the female.
There are many more males than females, which is why they court so extravagantly. It takes males several years to fully develop the feathers on their hood, which they use to attract a female.
When a beautiful bird of paradise opens its comb, the feathers resemble a smiling face.
Dart frogs feed on ants, mites, and termites. The degree of toxicity depends on this diet.
Arrow frogs can be found throughout Central and South America, mainly in rainforests. They move around on the ground, but also high in the treetops!
Their bright color is related to their toxicity, and thus deters potential predators!
They lay their eggs in moist places near leaves and roots. After hatching, the adult frogs carry their tadpoles to the water.
Arrow frogs got their name from natives who used the poisonous secretions of some species to ward off their arrows.

category.name: zoo.collectableItems.forgottenZoo
category.item:
Collect tools and exchange them for expansions for your hidden zoo.

You get tools in safari and on the wheel of fortune. Then take them to the shop and exchange them.

The current number of tools you own is:
The 3rd zoo expansion will give you access to the hidden zoo. It doesn't look like a very big expansion, but you can expand it using tools. And here's how it works:

1. Enter the hidden zoo, go to the store and click on "add-ons".
  2. You will get 2 cards to expand the zoo - one in exchange for tools, the other for zoodollars.
  3. Collect enough tools on the wheel of fortune or on safari and exchange them for a map.

Expand your zoo using both maps - simply with tools and zoodollars.
You own exactly as many tools as you need:

category.name: zoo.collectableItems.easterEvent
category.item: From April 21st to April 27th, you'll experience a flood of Easter eggs! Collect them and exchange them for Easter enclosure decorations, Easter bunnies, and enclosures. Your current Easter egg status:
